description: Ambitious and cunning head of the Rougon family, involved in political machinations to secure power in Plassans. Former oil merchant, he aims to capitalize on political upheaval for personal gain.
Pierre Rougon's astute and manipulative wife, deeply involved in the political intrigues of Plassans and seeks to secure her family's fortune.
Son of Pierre and Félicité, involved in the Bonapartist movement and provides political guidance to his family from Paris.
Son of Pierre and Félicité, a journalist with shifting political allegiances, initially a republican but opportunistic and seeks to align with the winning side.
Son of Pierre and Félicité, a doctor with a passion for science, living a modest life in Plassans, and helps the Macquart family during Antoine's illness.
Daughter of Pierre and Félicité, married to François Mouret and living in Marseille.
Daughter of Pierre and Félicité, married and living in Paris.
Son of Aristide and Angèle, sent to college by his grandmother Félicité.
Pierre Rougon's half-brother, a schemer with a grudge against the Rougons, involved in republican activities and hopes to take revenge on the Rougons.
Mother of Pierre and Antoine, known for her erratic behavior and scandalous relationship with Macquart. She suffers from a mental breakdown and remains detached from the political events.
A young, idealistic republican and grandson of Adélaïde. He is involved in the uprising and deeply in love with Miette, ultimately executed.
A young girl, deeply in love with Silvère, who joins the insurrection and carries the red flag. She is strong-willed and determined, but ultimately meets a tragic end.
Miette's father, a poacher sent to the penal colony for killing a gendarme.
Miette's uncle, with whom she lives. He is described as harsh and imposing hard work on Miette.
Miette's cousin, who harbors a deep hatred for her and insults her publicly.
Son of Antoine Macquart, a hardworking young man who eventually leaves his father due to his laziness and exploitation.
Daughter of Antoine Macquart, who is physically weak and eventually leaves with her partner Lantier.
Antoine Macquart's wife, a hardworking woman who supports the family until her death.
Silvère's father, who committed suicide after the death of his wife Ursule.
Silvère's mother, who died of a slow illness, leaving her children orphaned.
Silvère's older brother, a diligent and sober young man who works for his uncle Pierre Rougon.
Wife of Aristide Rougon, known for her appetite and love of flashy clothes.
A retired military officer and father-in-law of Aristide, he is a staunch Bonapartist and supports the Rougons' political ambitions.
An elderly nobleman with ties to the Rougon family, he is involved in the political intrigues of Plassans and supports the legitimist cause.
A retired almond merchant and member of the municipal council, he is a conservative and a regular at the Rougon's political gatherings.
A wealthy retired bonnetier and Orléanist, he is influential in Plassans and a key figure in the conservative circle around the Rougons.
A bookseller and publisher of the Gazette de Plassans, he is a conservative and supports the clergy's interests in the political landscape of Plassans.
The mayor of Plassans, a legitimist who is cautious about the political changes and maintains order in the town.
The deceased former tax collector, whose position Pierre Rougon hopes to obtain.
A blacksmith from Alboise who joins the insurgents.
A terrassier who follows Antoine Macquart's orders during the uprising.
A gendarme injured by Silvère during the uprising, who haunts Silvère's thoughts.
A military officer leading the troops against the insurgents.
The prefect of the department, accompanying the military forces against the insurgents.
A peasant from Poujols, executed alongside Silvère.
